acceptance.proof: A dependency-free allocation-free raw exchange contract admits exactly four finite f64 values in t0 through t3 role order, preserves their bits, and rejects the first non-finite input with a stable typed role.
A separate fallible evaluation computes RTT as (t3-t0)-(t2-t1) and OFS as ((t1-t0)+(t2-t3))/2, retaining the raw exchange unchanged.
Finite extreme inputs exercise every reachable non-finite subtraction/sum stage and return the first stable typed stage without panic or partial accepted result.

No packet shape, clock/timer read, I/O, UDP, retries, scheduling, filtering/minimum selection, offset history, correction/mapping, smoothing, dejitter, currentness, runtime activation, dependency, device, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

commit_policy: Commit the finite data/formula contract separately from lifecycle acceptance and push preparation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route LSLC-002M finite formula behavior and strict non-scope.
Synchronize the public numeric candidate and runtime exclusions.
Review public/private routing; no shared-skill mutation expected.
Review finite numeric evidence and closed runtime authority; no shared-skill mutation expected.

non_scope: Packet bytes or shapes, endpoint roles, clocks/timers, timestamp acquisition, I/O, sockets, UDP, retries, scheduling, periodic execution, or provider evidence.

Eight-exchange collection, filtering, minimum-RTT selection, offset history, timestamp correction/mapping, smoothing, dejitter, currentness, accuracy, reachability, interoperability, or runtime activation.
Dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, black-box observation, or Manifold authority.
objective: Add the smallest dependency-free allocation-free raw four-timestamp clock-exchange data and documented RTT/OFS formula contract justified by LSLC-002L, with explicit first-failure rejection of non-finite inputs and reachable non-finite intermediate/output arithmetic.
outputs: Finite raw t0/t1/t2/t3 value contract with bit-preserving access.
Fallible RTT/OFS formula result with stable intermediate-stage errors and valid/damaged tests.
Public routing/architecture/validation documentation, Standard receipt, and exact-ID executed no-force feature-push receipt.
